en_body:
Sun-smoked twilight...the market disperses
Late and forlorn, turning toward the waiting wharf
The setting sun lingers along the rising waves
A gentle wind stirs the wandering blue mist
A bell's echo returns from the wharf of remembrance
Moon through the ravine hangs, slipping past the summer roof
Sky, cloud, and water share one confidence
One Hsiao-Hsiang scene awakens the cradle of memories...
note: Hsiao-Hsiang, the rivers Xiao and Xiang in classical poetry, evokes distance, exile, and longing.
